docs.text: sec-filing-analyzer Production-ready starter backend for a Filing Intelligence platform that: tracks S&P 500 companies fetches SEC 10-Q filings through sec-api.io extracts MD&A and Risk Factors sections analyzes filing text with the OpenAI Responses API stores filings, sections, and AI output in PostgreSQL processes long-running work with Celery and Redis Prerequisites Docker Docker Compose sec-api.io API key OpenAI API key Quick Start Copy the environment template: cp .env.example .env Fill in OPENAI_API_KEY and SEC_API_KEY in .env . If you are using Gemini instead, set AI_PROVIDER=gemini , add GEMINI_API_KEY , and set a real SEC_USER_AGENT with your contact email. Start the stack: docker compose up Check the health endpoint: curl http://localhost:8000/health Expected response: {"status":"ok"} Open the dashboard: http://localhost:8000/ Discover Filings Create a company if needed, search for 10-Q filings, and store them: curl -X POST http://localhost:8000/filings/discover \ -H "Content-Type: application/json" \ -d '{ "ticker": "AAPL", "cik": "0000320193", "start_date": "2025-01-01", "end_date": "2025-12-31" }' End-to-End Flow POST /filings/discover POST /filings/<filing_id>/analyze GET /filings/<filing_id> The Celery task: loads the filing from PostgreSQL extracts part1item2 and part2item1a from sec-api.io stores the sections in filing_sections sends the combined filing context to OpenAI gpt-5.4-mini stores the structured AI output in ai_runs
docs.title: Home

If you are using Gemini instead, set AI_PROVIDER=gemini , add GEMINI_API_KEY , and set a real SEC_USER_AGENT with your contact email. Start the stack: docker compose up Check the health endpoint: curl http://localhost:8000/health Expected response: {"status":"ok"} Open the dashboard: http://localhost:8000/
